{{Infobox group
| name = Gardeners
| introduced = Of Shadow and Sea
}}

''See also: [[Gardeners Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Gardeners are a secretive order of assassins within the [[Imperial]] [[Guild]]'s [[Council of Architects]]. Although they are officially denied to those outside the [[Island]], they serve a vital role in maintaining stability within the [[Empire]] by acting as an [[Imperial]] [[Guild]] that settles disputes, preventing the rise of private armies and fractured nations. Within the hierarchy of the [[Architects]], they are tasked with 'removing weeds,' a metaphor for their role in pruning unnecessary elements to preserve balance. While other members of the [[Council]], such as [[Masons]] or [[Shepherds]], focus on infiltration or observation, Gardeners specialize in lethal assignments. Members of this order are highly skilled and often difficult to detect, possessing a level of competence that can intimidate even seasoned warriors. They are trained from a young age, with some members being as young as twelve. The leadership of the order is held by the [[High Gardener]], a position currently occupied by [[Kerian]]. Most Gardeners retire from active duty by age fifty to transition into other roles within the [[Architects]]. Each [[Gardener]] is equipped with a pair of shears, which are ancient bronze daggers imbued with significant amounts of [[Intent]]. These weapons are capable of easily piercing bone and armor, making them highly effective tools for assassination. Because of their specialized training and the nature of their work, Gardeners are often viewed with a mixture of respect and fear.}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|The Gardeners are a secret group whose existence is denied to the public, though they have worked in shadows for most of [[Meia]]'s [[life]]. Originally an order that provided strategic advice concerning the [[Soulbound]], their primary purpose is to maintain balance within the [[Empire]] and spread [[Nakothi]]'s influence. While they are trained to avoid armed conflict and possess shears that cannot withstand a [[sword]], the group holds the power to be sent to depopulate [[The Testament]]. Though [[Lucan]] once studied this discipline, only three Gardeners currently exist, including the two being utilized by [[Yala]] and a trio traveling alongside [[the Emperor]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Yala]] is currently using two [[Gardeners]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c2">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Kerian]] wondered if [[Maxwell]] was imitating the [[Gardeners]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c3">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gardeners]] are trained to avoid armed conflict.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c6">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gardeners]] are a group that the public is told does not exist.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c7">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|The original order of [[Gardeners]] provided strategic advice regarding [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c8">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|The shears of the [[Gardeners]] are not designed to hold their own against a [[sword]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|The purpose of [[Gardeners]] is to maintain balance in the [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c9">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|The [[Gardeners]] worked in secret for most of [[Meia]]'s [[life]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c15">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gardeners]] spread [[Nakothi]]'s influence.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c17">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|There are three [[Gardeners]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c18">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gardeners]] is a group or discipline [[Lucan]] studied.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c19">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|There are three [[Gardeners]] traveling with [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c20">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gardeners]] are a group that could be sent to depopulate [[The Testament]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c27">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kern]] mentions the existence of [[Gardeners]] as a group to fight against.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c18">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The Gardeners are a sparsely populated guild squad within the [[Empire]], recognizable by their standard-issue black clothing. This group follows commands from an unnamed leader and performs pruning work as decided by the [[Council]], which includes removing weeds and eliminating threats to their guild. Members such as [[Meia]], [[Shera]], and [[Lucan]] undergo training provided by [[the Emperor]], having previously been part of an instructional program led by [[Ayana]]. The group's status is significant enough to be used in comparisons regarding value, though their legitimacy has even faced inspection by the [[Readers]] regarding imitations.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardeners]] are a group that can be recognized by those in the [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c2">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Gardeners]] are a guild that is sparsely populated.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c3">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Gardeners]] remove weeds.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardeners]] wear standard-issue blacks.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c4">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardeners]] are a group mentioned in comparison to [[Shera]]'s value.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Gardeners]] include [[Meia]] and [[Shera]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c7">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Gardeners]] are a group that follows commands from an unnamed leader.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Gardeners]] are a group that [[Ayana]] was an instructor in.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c8">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardeners]] remove threats to their [[Guild]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardeners]] wear black clothing known as [[Gardener blacks]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c10">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardeners]] are trained by [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c12">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardeners]] are a squad within the [[Guild]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c13">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Shera]], [[Meia]], and [[Lucan]] are [[Gardeners]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c14">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Readers]] inspected imitation [[Gardeners]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c17">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The [[Gardeners]] are a group that performs pruning work decided by the [[Council]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c22">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Gardeners]] are a group that has been acting against [[Calder]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c13">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Gardeners]] are a group that might have stabbed the [[Steward]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The Gardeners are an order of [[Consultants]] led by [[Guild Head]] [[Shera]], distinguished by their use of skintight black silks. Within the guild, only the fifteen most senior members are awarded true [[Gardener]] shears, some of which glow, and members typically retire or transition to the [[Architects]] by age fifty. While [[Shera]] maintains imitation members of the group, she notably trusts plans less than she trusts the Gardeners. During recent events, [[Shera]] deployed the order to spread throughout the city and infiltrate crowds alongside her associates. The group has been involved in assassination attempts against certain [[Champions]], and [[the Emperor]]'s personal Gardeners were the sole individuals aware of the full details regarding the events in his chambers.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Gardeners]] are an order of [[Consultant]] that uses skintight black silks as symbols.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c2">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Gardeners]] are a group that [[Shera]] has 'baby' or imitation members of.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c4">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Gardeners]] are a guild that some [[Consultants]] have opinions about.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c9">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Only fifteen sets of true [[Gardener]] shears remain in the [[Guild]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c10">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The fifteen most senior [[Gardeners]] are awarded shears.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Gardeners]] typically retire or move to the [[Architects]] by fifty.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Gardeners]] are a group that [[Shera]] does not trust plans as much as.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Gardeners]] are a group that [[Shera]] is associated with and who attempt to assassinate certain [[Champions]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c16">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|A handful of [[Gardeners]] infiltrated the crowd with [[Shera]] and [[Jorin]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c18">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[The Emperor]]'s personal [[Gardeners]] were the only ones who knew the full details of the events in his chambers.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c19">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Gardeners]] are present at [[Shera]]'s meeting.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Shera]] had sent [[Gardeners]] to spread out among the city.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c23">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The [[Gardeners]] are a group led by [[Guild Head]] [[Shera]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c24">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Shera]] calls upon the [[Gardeners]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Some [[Gardeners]] carry glowing shears.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c26">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|"They remove threats to their Guild, son, As you do for us, though I'm certain they operate on another level entirely."<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c8" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire groups]]
